title: End-to-end model card for one pilot open cancer model
context: The pilot proves the end-to-end pipeline from gate through authoring to acceptance and is the final M0 deliverable. It must use a model and acceptance path selected for realistic delivery: either an informal channel (author agreed to review a model-card PR) or a self-serve Zenodo fallback. No pilot model is selected until outreach-008 provides the shortlist. Depends on schema-001, gate-003, ncpolicy-004, bias-005, validator-006, reviewer-002, and outreach-008.
objective: Produce a complete, source-verified model card for one pilot open cancer ML model that has passed the cancer-data gate, and get it accepted by the model's host via an informal channel or Zenodo self-serve fallback, with the acceptance evidence artifact recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: Pilot model selected from outreach-008 shortlist on a realistic acceptance path (informal channel or Zenodo self-serve fallback) — documented before authoring begins.
Model and every dataset passed gate-003 (open/de-identified; license permits use) with the committed gate artifact; controlled-access/identifiable data confirmed absent.

Complete card produced using the canonical schema (schema-001): intended use, out-of-scope use, data provenance, headline and subgroup metrics (reported or gap-flagged per bias-005), limitations, clinical-validation status, regulatory status, and the not-medical-advice disclaimer; every claim cited in citations[].
Card validated by the schema validator (validator-006) and approved by the Data-License+Privacy reviewer and the ML/technical reviewer.
Provenance and license snapshots recorded (committed copy + SHA-256 + Wayback) for the model artifact and each dataset.
Completeness score before/after recorded; after score >= 90/100.
Card accepted via the informal channel or Zenodo self-serve fallback with the Steward's outcomes/<model-id>.json acceptance artifact recorded — or submitted with the blocker surfaced.
resources: PLAN.md — Pipeline (per model), M0 cold-start de-risking, Definition of Shipped
Mitchell et al., Model Cards for Model Reporting (2019)
output: A complete model card for the pilot open cancer ML model, committed to the project repo, submitted to the model host, and accompanied by the Steward's outcomes/<model-id>.json acceptance artifact.
